as: Revenue recognized when risks and rewards are transferred and income is earned and realizable.
indAs: Revenue recognized when control of goods or services transfers to the customer, based on a five-step model.
ifrs: Same five-step control-based model as Ind AS; principle-based.
usGaap: Same five-step model as IFRS but with more detailed US guidance.
logicShift: From 'earned/realized' and risks & rewards to control and performance obligations.

as: Limited explicit focus on 'contract with customer'; emphasis on individual transactions and invoicing.
indAs: Explicit definition of contract with customer with enforceable rights and obligations.
ifrs: Same as Ind AS; strong contract-based framework.
usGaap: Same as IFRS; legal contract and enforceable rights drive analysis.
logicShift: Shift from invoice driven to contract driven analysis.

as: Revenue when significant risks and rewards are transferred, collection certain. Usually at invoice/contract price, adjusted for known rebates.
indAs: Identify contract and performance obligations, recognize revenue when/as obligations are satisfied. Measure transaction price, estimate variables.
ifrs: Same model; revenue over time or at a point in time based on control transfer. Same transaction price model.
usGaap: Same model and criteria; more detailed application guidance. Explicit treatment of variables & financing.
logicShift: From a single event (delivery) to a pattern of performance. From 'billed value' to transaction price estimation.

as: Limited concept of subsequent re-measurement except for provisions/discounts; revenue rarely revisited.
indAs: Adjust revenue prospectively if estimates (variable consideration, progress, etc.) change.
ifrs: Same; high reliance on updates of estimates.
usGaap: Same; strong use of updated estimates and cumulative catch-up adjustments.
logicShift: From static to dynamic measurement linked to updated estimates.

basisOfComparison: Over time vs point in time
as: No formal over-time model; long-term contracts mainly via AS 7/AS 9 with completion method or POC in limited cases.
indAs: Distinct criteria to determine over time vs point in time; cost-to-cost or output methods to measure progress.
usGaap: Same; extensive guidance for construction/long-term arrangements.
logicShift: Introduces systematic over-time recognition linked to performance progress.

as: Sparse guidance; often split by legal contract/milestone; risk of arbitrary allocation.
indAs: Identify distinct performance obligations and allocate transaction price based on standalone selling prices.
usGaap: Same; lots of industry examples (software, telecom, etc.).
logicShift: From ad-hoc allocation to principled allocation based on standalone prices.

as: Limited and conservative; often recognized only when no significant uncertainty.
indAs: Estimate variable consideration (discounts, penalties) and apply constraint to avoid significant revenue reversal.
usGaap: Same; specific guidance for royalties, sales-or usage-based fees.
logicShift: Introduces probability-weighted / expected value thinking with explicit constraint.

as: Advances and unbilled revenue shown, but not under a unified conceptual framework.
indAs: Explicit contract assets (unbilled rights) and contract liabilities (deferred revenue).
usGaap: Same; terminology similar ('contract asset' and 'contract liability').
logicShift: From generic advances/unbilled to structured contract positions.

as: Limited, less developed guidance; focus on legal form and risk.
indAs: Control-based principal vs agent assessment (gross vs net revenue).
logicShift: From legal/title focus to control and exposure analysis.

as: Not systematically addressed; interest element often not separated unless explicit.
indAs: Identify and adjust for significant financing component in transaction price when cash timing diverges.
usGaap: Same; interest income/expense split is explicit.
logicShift: From ignoring time value to explicit financing vs revenue split.

indAs: Measure at fair value and include in transaction price.
usGaap: Same; measure at fair value, or indirectly by reference to standalone selling price.
logicShift: From 'billed cash only' mindset to full economic consideration.

as: Treated as revision of contract terms with limited principle-level guidance.
indAs: Distinct rules for separate contracts vs modifications, depending on scope/price.
logicShift: From ad-hoc to structured modification accounting.

indAs: Options for full retrospective or modified retrospective adoption.
usGaap: Similar full or modified retrospective transition.

as: Limited requirements, high-level splits by broad categories only.
indAs: Extensive narrative. Disaggregation by type, geography, market, timing, and contract balances.
ifrs: Similar depth and themes as Ind AS. Same disaggregation requirements.
usGaap: Equally comprehensive; SEC overlays add more granularity.
logicShift: From minimal to multi-dimensional revenue analytics in notes.

indAs: Substantially converged with IFRS 15, with limited Indian carve-outs.
usGaap: Not word-for-word converged, but outcome converged in most contracts.
logicShift: Global move to a unified revenue logic.
